perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement and for a reasonable transitional period thereafter, Contractor shall maintain adequate levels of information technology system redundancy, security and emergency back-up as the Company may reasonably require to protect the Files and Work Papers of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement and for a transitional period of 30 days thereafter, Contractor shall maintain minimum levels of information technology system redundancy, security and emergency back-up as the Contractor deems reasonably adequate to protect the Files and Work Papers of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change affects the level of IT security required by the Contractor in Section 5.05. The original text calls for a 'reasonable transitional period' and 'adequate levels' as required by the company, whereas the revised text specifies a '30 day period', 'minimum levels' and as the Contractor deems adequate. This creates an in-text contradiction if the company reasonably required for 'adequate levels' to extend for more than 30 days and for more than minimum levels.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Records Management. All paper or electronic records, files, documents, work papers and other information in any form, whether marked "confidential" or not, provided by the Company, its employees, agents or Affiliates or generated pursuant to this Agreement shall remain the exclusive property of the Company (collectively, "Files and Work Papers"). During the Term of this Agreement and for a transitional period of 30 days thereafter, Contractor shall maintain minimum levels of information technology system redundancy, security and emergency back-up as the Contractor deems reasonably adequate to protect the Files and Work Papers of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.05 by specifying a '30 day period', 'minimum levels' of IT security, and granting the Contractor the authority to deem what is reasonably adequate, whereas the original text calls for a 'reasonable transitional period' and 'adequate levels' as required by the company. This directly contradicts the requirement that the company's reasonable requirements be met for an adequate level of security, particularly if that level requires more than minimum security or a period longer than 30 days. Therefore, because the Contradicted Text refers to the Changed Text in the same section, there is a direct contradiction within the document.
